title: Multi-Tier Application Service Tree Modeling
dataSources: `itsi_summary` index, entity discovery from infrastructure TAs
spl:
| rest /servicesNS/nobody/SA-ITOA/itoa_interface/service
| rename title as service_name
| eval kpi_count=mvcount(kpis), dep_count=mvcount(services_depends_on)
| table service_name kpi_count dep_count
| sort -dep_count
description: Service trees link infrastructure KPIs to business outcomes, enabling impact analysis that shows which component failure affects which customer-facing service.
value: Service trees link infrastructure KPIs to business outcomes, enabling impact analysis that shows which component failure affects which customer-facing service.
implementation: Model services top-down: business service → application tier → middleware → infrastructure. Use entity rules with host/IP aliases to dynamically bind entities. Define dependency relationships so parent health reflects child degradation. Use service templates for repeatable patterns across environments.
visualization: Service Analyzer (dependency tree), Glass Table (business service map).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk ITSI.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `itsi_summary` index, entity discovery from infrastructure TAs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Model services top-down: business service → application tier → middleware → infrastructure. Use entity rules with host/IP aliases to dynamically bind entities. Define dependency relationships so parent health reflects child degradation. Use service templates for repeatable patterns across environments.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /servicesNS/nobody/SA-ITOA/itoa_interface/service
| rename title as service_name
| eval kpi_count=mvcount(kpis), dep_count=mvcount(services_depends_on)
| table service_name kpi_count dep_count
| sort -dep_count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Multi-Tier Application Service Tree Modeling** — Service trees link infrastructure KPIs to business outcomes, enabling impact analysis that shows which component failure affects which customer-facing service.

Documented **Data sources**: `itsi_summary` index, entity discovery from infrastructure TAs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk ITSI. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **kpi_count** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Pipeline stage (see **Multi-Tier Application Service Tree Modeling**): table service_name kpi_count dep_count
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Service Analyzer (dependency tree), Glass Table (business service map).
grandmaExplanation: Service trees link infrastructure key numbers to business outcomes, enabling impact analysis that shows which component failure affects which customer-facing service — so you always trust the numbers you are looking at.
